rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical intramolecular cyclization of 2-alkenyl-substituted arenes (benzoic acids, benzamides, phenols, anilines) to give phthalides, isoindolinones, benzofurans, and indoles via anodic oxidation of the alkene to a radical cation followed by heteroatom cyclization.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the styrenyl alkene to a radical cation that is intercepted by a tethered nucleophile (CO2-, CONHR, OH, NHTs); for 2-alkenylbenzoic acid 1a a prior cathodic reduction generates the carboxylate (Int-1a) with H2 evolution before anodic oxidation.

key_intermediates.name: benzylic cation (Int-1d) trapped by methoxide to give phthalide 2a

electron_flow: For the phthalide manifold, the cathode first reduces the carboxylic acid (H2 evolution) to its carboxylate, which is then anodically oxidized at the alkene to a radical cation; 5-exo (or 6-endo in HFIP) O-cyclization gives a benzylic radical that is further oxidized at the anode to a cation and trapped by MeOH/methoxide. For benzamide, phenol, and N-Ts aniline substrates, direct anodic oxidation of the styrene gives a radical cation, intramolecular N- or O-attack with loss of H+ affords a cyclic C-radical, which is oxidized again to a cation (or in the benzamide case undergoes HAT with MeCN) and loses H+ to give the aromatic isoindolinone/benzofuran/indole; in the benzofuran case tosylate is incorporated then β-eliminated.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: All four reactions inhibited; no TEMPO adduct detected by MS.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reactions inhibited; trace product only; no BHT adduct detected.

probe_reagents.name: acetonitrile-d3
probe_reagents.observation: 50% D incorporation into the CH2 of isoindolinone 5a, supporting HAT from MeCN to Int-4b.

probe_reagents.name: (E)-stilbene
probe_reagents.observation: Gives vic-bis(tosyloxy)stilbene 12 in 23% under standard conditions with Et4NOTs, supporting tosylate trapping of an alkene radical cation.

probe_reagents.name: Koser reagent (PhI(OH)OTs, 13)
probe_reagents.observation: Stoichiometric addition to 7a (no current) afforded 2-phenylbenzofuran 8a in 31%, mimicking the proposed tosyloxy-cation intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: 2,4,6-collidine
probe_reagents.observation: Non-oxidizable base; addition to 1a generates a new oxidation wave at 1.10 V vs SCE, indicating that the deprotonated carboxylate is the species being anodically oxidized.

probe_reagents.name: current off (no electrolysis)
probe_reagents.observation: No reaction without electric current, confirming the electrochemical origin of the radical intermediates.

notes: Selectivity between 5-exo (phthalide 2a, in MeOH) and 6-endo (isochromanone 3a, in HFIP) cyclization of Int-1b is unexplained; both Path A and Path B are retained as possibilities. For benzofuran formation, Path B (tosylate-assisted) is favored over Path A (direct cyclization of Int-7a) because Et4NOTs dramatically raises yield (12% → 62%) and stilbene/Koser control experiments support OTs incorporation, but Path A cannot be excluded. The unexpected formation of isoindolin-1-one 5h in DMSO (a poor H-atom donor) suggests an alternative non-HAT pathway in that case. Chemoselectivity between C–N and C–O bond formation for 2-alkenylbenzamides in MeOH (giving iminoisobenzofurans 6) vs MeCN (giving isoindolinones 5) is not yet explained.
